title: coreopsis stillmanii
intro: Coreopsis stillmanii, also known as the Stillman's Tickseed, is a perennial plant species belonging to the daisy family, characterized by its bright yellow flowers and slender stems. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial properties, making it effective in treating wounds, infections, and skin conditions. Coreopsis stillmanii has been traditionally used to promote overall health and wellness, with reported benefits including reducing fever, treating digestive issues, and supporting immune function. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, tinctures, or infusions, often used to calm digestive issues, alleviate respiratory problems, and provide a natural remedy for various health concerns.
